hawkish_phrases: an ongoing tightening of monetary policy over the forecast period...would be appropriate
excess demand builds over the second half of the forecast period
inflation settles at a rate a little above the target
gently rising path of Bank Rate implied by market yields
dovish_phrases: UK economic growth slowed in late 2018 and appears to have weakened further in early 2019
world economy has continued to slow over recent months
weaker near-term outlook is likely to lead to a small margin of slack opening up this year
CPI inflation...is expected to decline to slightly below the MPC's 2% target in the near term
monetary policy response to Brexit...could be in either direction
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.75%, with guidance that ongoing gradual tightening would be appropriate if the economy develops in line with projections showing inflation settling above target and excess demand building.
